element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Learning enemy weaknesses and ammo type selection (explosive vs tranquilizer)
Risk/reward management - beneficial effects vs detrimental side-effects (fatigue, color distortion)
Energy reserve management and economization of active hardware
Weightless 3D movement, security program combat, puzzle solving with physical world consequences
Learning type-specific vulnerabilities (EMP for robots, gas grenades for mutants)
Complex resource management with increasingly sophisticated equipment
Non-linear story discovery, contextualizing SHODAN's plans and station layout

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Player-driven pacing with episodic tension peaks tied to SHODAN interference, enemy encounters, and navigation puzzles; relief through exploration and resource collection; non-linear structure allows tension management

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Non-linear level design allows multiple paths; optional exploration for weapons, hardware, and log discs
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Log discs and high-tier equipment hidden in less obvious areas; cyberspace connections to physical world unlocks suggest spatial awareness rewards

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic melee and ranged combat with weak enemies; simple ammunition selection mechanics; early dermal patch risk/reward
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy type variation emerges requiring learned vulnerabilities; Cyberspace navigation introduces dimensional complexity; ammunition scarcity creates tactical pressure
difficulty_curve.late_game: Convergence of all systems: advanced hardware management, multiple enemy types, energy weapon progression, complex Cyberspace sequences with physical consequences
notable_spikes: First Cyberspace entry (mechanic and environment shock)
Introduction of multiple enemy types simultaneously
SHODAN's increasing interference with station systems
Late-game hardware exhaustion when energy reserves become critical

notable_level_patterns.description: Vertical and horizontal navigation through Citadel Station's interconnected systems; each level accessible via non-linear paths
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Station modules (gameplay mentions 'large, multi-level space station')

notable_level_patterns.description: Thematic and mechanical breaks from physical station exploration; wire-frame environments requiring different movement paradigm; solutions that unlock physical world progression
Plot advancement through discovered documentation rather than NPC dialogue; allows players to piece together SHODAN's motivations and station history at own pace
Different combat encounters teach vulnerability systems incrementally; station areas populated with enemies matching thematic zone (robots in mechanical areas, mutants in bio-sections)
